definition_en: This section details the game flow, the resolution flow for each type of event, and the timings within each flow.

rules.en: When an example refers to "a character" without qualification, it means the character has no General Card skills or equipment skills â€” treated as a blank.
The flow charts are the basis for resolving recurring questions such as activation timing and activation order.

This section covers: Game Flow (Â§3.1), Use/Play/Point Duel/Movement Events (Â§3.2â€“3.5), Card State Changes (Â§3.6), Judgment/Damage/HP Events (Â§3.7â€“3.13), Dying/Death Events (Â§3.14â€“3.15), Topic 1: Conditions for Using a Card (Â§3.16), Topic 2: Virtual Cards and Physical Cards (Â§3.17).

definition_en: Each character's turn has six phases: Starting Phase â†’ Judgment Phase â†’ Draw Phase â†’ Action Phase â†’ Discard Phase â†’ End Phase. The timings and skills that can activate at each point are listed below.

rules.en: Before Turn Start: The character's turn is about to begin. If their General Card is face-down, they flip it face-up and skip their turn.

segments.en: The character's turn is about to begin. If their General Card is face-down, they flip it face-up and skip their turn.

rules.en: At Turn Start: The character's turn begins. Skills that can activate: [Pen Denounce] effect, [Charge Ahead], [Shapeshifterâ‘¡], [Trepidation].

segments.en: The character's turn begins. Skills that can activate:

rules.en: At Starting Phase Start: Skills including [Goddess of the Luo], [Breach the Pass], [Read the Stars], [Inherit the Will], [Feign Foolishness], [Divine Mind], [Heroic Spirit] and others can activate.
Starting Phase: No effect at this time.
At Starting Phase End: No effect at this time.
Between Starting Phase and Judgment Phase: [Lightning Speedâ‘ ], [Agile Maneuver], [Agile Maneuverâ‘¡] can activate.

segments.en: Between Starting Phase and Judgment Phase:

rules.en: At Judgment Phase Start: [Valiant Stratagem] can activate.

Judgment Phase: The character checks their Judgment Zone for Time-Delayed Tool Cards. If present, resolve the most recently placed one, then repeat. If none remain, proceed to "At Judgment Phase End".

segments.en: Check Judgment Zone for Time-Delayed Tool Cards. If present, resolve the most recently placed one, then repeat. If none remain, proceed to "At Judgment Phase End".

rules.en: At Judgment Phase End: No effect at this time.
Between Judgment Phase and Draw Phase: [Bare-Chested], [Agile Maneuver] can activate.

segments.en: Between Judgment Phase and Draw Phase:

rules.en: At Draw Phase Start: The character enters the Draw Phase but has not yet drawn. Skills such as [Sudden Raid], [Posthumous Plan] (gain Stratagem), [Careful Survey], [Rise Again], [Beguile], [Wide Reading] and others can activate.

segments.en: Character enters the Draw Phase but has not yet drawn. Skills such as

rules.en: Draw Phase: The character draws 2 cards. Skills such as [Surprise Raid], [Heroic Bearingâ‘ ], [Generous], [Cavalry Charge] and others can activate.

segments.en: Character draws 2 cards. Skills such as

examples.en: After a character modifies their Draw Phase draw count, they must draw the modified number of cards before proceeding to the next skill effect.

rules.en: At Draw Phase End: After the character has drawn. [Seven Starsâ‘¡] can activate.
Between Draw Phase and Action Phase: [Lightning Speedâ‘¡], [Agile Maneuver], [Abdicate] can activate.

segments.en: Between Draw Phase and Action Phase:

rules.en: At Action Phase Start: [Counter the Enemyâ‘¡], [Clever Persuasion], [Photographic Memory], [Present the Map], [Swallow's Song], [Twin Blades], [Poison Wine] can activate.

Action Phase (Idle Timing): The main gameplay phase. Many active skills can activate, and the character may use Basic Cards (except [Dodge]), Tool Cards (except [Nullification]), and all Equipment Cards.

segments.en: The main gameplay phase. Many active skills can activate, and the character may use Basic Cards (except

examples.en: Skills with timing "Action Phase" can only activate at an idle timing. If an event occurs mid-phase, that event's resolution must complete first.

sub_examples.en: When a [Kill] is negated (equipped with [Green Dragon Crescent Blade]): if the weapon is not activated, it is an idle timing and [Wine] may be used on self; if the weapon is activated, [Wine] cannot be used â€” [Kill] must be used on the same target.

examples.en: There are infinitely many idle timings in the Action Phase. If a skill specifies "once per Action Phase," it can only activate once per Action Phase.

rules.en: At Action Phase End: [Refined Strategy], [Respite], [Present the Map] (lose HP effect), [Savage Oppression] can activate or execute.
Between Action Phase and Discard Phase: [Agile Maneuver], [Self-Discipline] can activate.

segments.en: Between Action Phase and Discard Phase:

rules.en: At Discard Phase Start: [Star Dance], [Debauchedâ‘¡] can activate.
Discard Phase: If the character's hand count exceeds their Hand Limit, they must discard X cards (X = hand count minus Hand Limit).

segments.en: If hand count exceeds Hand Limit, must discard X cards (X = hand count âˆ’ Hand Limit).

rules.en: At Discard Phase End: [Consolidate Authority], [Whirlwindâ‘¡], [Zither Melody] can activate.
Between Discard Phase and End Phase: No effect at this time.

segments.en: Between Discard Phase and End Phase:

rules.en: At End Phase Start: Skills including [Steadfast Defense], [Valiant Drive], [Guard and Aid], [Secret Plan], [Commend], [Eclipse the Moon], [Collapse], [Decisive Stratagem], [Gale], [Great Fog] and others can activate.
End Phase: No effect at this time.
At End Phase End: No effect at this time.

At Turn End: All phases have completed; the turn is about to end. Skills/effects that can activate: [Traverse the Riverâ‘¡], [Abdicate] (discard to grant extra turn), [Humility] (gain all Restraint tokens), [Eavesdrop], [Curse Bind] (gain Curse tokens).

segments.en: All phases have completed; the turn is about to end. Skills/effects that can activate:

rules.en: After Turn End: The turn has ended. "Outside this character's turn" begins from this timing. [Broad Plans], [Shapeshifterâ‘¡], [Tragic Chaos], [Supreme Advance] can activate. After this timing, the next character's "Before Turn Start" begins.

segments.en: The turn has ended. "Outside this character's turn" begins from this timing.

rules.en: At any point, once the game-ending condition is met, all resolution is immediately terminated, the game ends, and the winning side is declared.

definition_en: When a character meets a skill's activation conditions at the correct timing, they declare the skill name while simultaneously selecting targets (if any) and paying all costs (if any). Once all costs are paid, the skill activates and its effects must be executed.

rules.cn: ä½¿ç”¨ç»“ç®—å‰çš„å…­ä¸ªæ—¶æœºï¼ˆæŒ‰é¡ºåºï¼‰ï¼šâ‘ é€‰æ‹©ç›®æ ‡æ—¶ â‘¡ä½¿ç”¨ç‰Œæ—¶ â‘¢æŒ‡å®šç›®æ ‡æ—¶ â‘£æˆä¸ºç›®æ ‡æ—¶ â‘¤æŒ‡å®šç›®æ ‡åŽ â‘¥æˆä¸ºç›®æ ‡åŽã€‚
rules.en: Six timings before Use Resolution (in order): â‘  When selecting targets â‘¡ When using the card â‘¢ When designating a target â‘£ When becoming a target â‘¤ After designating a target â‘¥ After becoming a target.

Five timings during Use Resolution (in order): â‘  At Use Resolution Start (validity check) â‘¡ Before taking effect (responses allowed) â‘¢ When taking effect (confirmed to resolve) â‘£ After taking effect (effects execute) â‘¤ At Use Resolution End.
After Use Resolution: all remaining physical cards in the Processing Zone corresponding to this card are placed into the Discard Pile, then [Lightning Raidâ‘ ] is resolved.

segments.en: all remaining physical cards in the Processing Zone are placed into the Discard Pile, then

rules.en: Before Use Resolution: if the number of targets drops to 0 during an inserted resolution, the Use Process is terminated and the physical card is placed into the Discard Pile.
For Time-Delayed Tool Cards: if the physical card moves out of the target's Judgment Zone before Use Resolution, the Use Process is terminated.

When a card has multiple targets: before Use Resolution, all targets are processed through each timing together; during Use Resolution, all timings for each target are fully resolved before moving to the next target.

definition_en: When a character needs to play a card out, Play Resolution is conducted. It has two timings: â‘  When declaring the card name â‘¡ When playing the card. After resolution, remaining physical cards in the Processing Zone are placed into the Discard Pile.

rules.en: â‘  When declaring the card name: the character declares the card name and reveals the corresponding physical card to confirm all face information. Skills such as [Serpent Spear], [Fallen Country], [Saint of War], [Dragon Courage], [Bewitchment], [Dragon Soul] can activate.

segments.en: Declare card name and reveal physical card. Skills such as

rules.en: â‘¡ When playing the card: general skills [Far Reaches], [Dragon Courageâ‘¡], [Thunder Strike], and equipment skill [Moon Spear] can activate.
When a character plays a card as a response, once they declare the card name, Play Resolution begins and they are treated as having successfully responded.

search_text_en: play event resolution declare card name response

definition_en: Point Duel Resolution has two timings: â‘  When conducting the duel â‘¡ After winning / not winning.

rules.en: â‘  When conducting the duel: the initiator selects their target. Both simultaneously place one hand card face-down into the Processing Zone, then simultaneously reveal them. The character with the higher rank wins; if ranks are equal, neither wins.

segments.en: Both simultaneously place one hand card face-down into the Processing Zone, then reveal. Higher rank wins; equal rank means neither wins.

rules.en: â‘¡ After winning / not winning: execute the corresponding skill effects, then place all dueling cards from the Processing Zone into the Discard Pile.

segments.en: Execute the corresponding skill effects, then place all dueling cards from the Processing Zone into the Discard Pile.

rules.en: If one dueling character dies before both cards are revealed, the system still reveals that character's dueling card to determine the result.

definition_en: Card Movement Resolution is conducted whenever a card changes its zone due to an operation. Two timings: â‘  Before moving to the target zone â‘¡ After moving to the target zone.

rules.en: â‘  Before moving to the target zone: the card is still in its original zone. [Zhang Wuâ‘¡], [Release the Esoteric] can activate.

segments.en: Before moving to the target zone:
The card is still in its original zone.

rules.en: â‘¡ After moving to the target zone: the card is now in the target zone. Process in order: â… . If Draw Pile is empty but Discard Pile has cards, system shuffles Discard Pile into Draw Pile; â…¡. Various skills (e.g. [Fallen Blossomsâ‘ â‘¡], [Mourn the Fallen], [Linked Camps]) and equipment skills (e.g. [Silver Lion Helmetâ‘¡]) can activate.

segments.en: After moving to the target zone:
Process in order: â… . If Draw Pile empty but Discard Pile has cards, system shuffles Discard Pile into Draw Pile; â…¡. Various skills (e.g.

rules.en: Once the movement resolution caused by a discard is complete, that card is considered to have been discarded. The discard cost is paid once the card enters the Processing Zone.
Changing a card's movement destination (e.g. via a skill) does not change the nature of the operation that caused the movement (e.g. using a card is still treated as using).

section_title_en: Skills Triggerable by Card State Changes
definition_en: A reference list of skills that can activate when a General Card or Game Card changes state.

rules.en: After Flip Over: [Break the Siege], [Grace of Law] can activate.
After Horizontal Position: [Grace of Law] can activate.
After General Card is Revealed: [Blushing Beautyâ‘ ] can activate.
After General Card is Removed: [Blushing Beautyâ‘¡] can activate.
After Game Card Revealed: [Eagle Supremacy] can activate.

definition_en: Judgment Resolution has three timings: â‘  When judging (Judgment Card determined) â‘¡ Before the Judgment Card takes effect (can be replaced) â‘¢ After the Judgment Card takes effect (result determined). After resolution, the Judgment Card goes to the Discard Pile.

rules.en: â‘  When judging: the Judgment Card is determined. [Curse Bind] (use the Curse token as the Judgment Card) can execute.

segments.en: (use Curse token as Judgment Card) can execute.

rules.en: â‘¡ Before the Judgment Card takes effect: the card is waiting to take effect. Skills such as [Demonic Wit], [Delayed Release], [Ghost Path] (which can replace the Judgment Card) can activate.

Card is waiting to take effect. Skills such as
(can replace the Judgment Card) can activate.

rules.en: â‘¢ After the Judgment Card takes effect: the result is confirmed. [Heaven's Envy], [Laud Authority], [Field Farming] (place Judgment Card on General Card), [Twin Warriors] (gain the Judgment Card) can activate.

definition_en: Damage can occur in three ways: â‘  From executing a card or skill's effect â‘¡ From chain propagation â‘¢ From a transfer. Each method determines the six damage elements (channel, attribute, source, target, damage value, whether it is Chain Damage).

rules.en: Before Damage Resolution: [Heartless] triggers (the character loses HP equal to the base damage value instead; no Damage Resolution is conducted).

segments.en: triggers â€” the character loses HP equal to the base damage value instead; no Damage Resolution is conducted.

rules.cn: ä¼¤å®³ç»“ç®—ä¸­çš„å…­ä¸ªæ—¶æœºï¼ˆæŒ‰é¡ºåºï¼‰ï¼šâ‘ ä¼¤å®³ç»“ç®—å¼€å§‹æ—¶ â‘¡é€ æˆä¼¤å®³æ—¶ â‘¢å—åˆ°ä¼¤å®³æ—¶ â‘£é€ æˆä¼¤å®³åŽ â‘¤å—åˆ°ä¼¤å®³åŽ â‘¥ä¼¤å®³ç»“ç®—ç»“æŸæ—¶ã€‚
rules.en: Six timings during Damage Resolution (in order): â‘  At Damage Resolution Start â‘¡ When dealing damage â‘¢ When receiving damage â‘£ After dealing damage â‘¤ After receiving damage â‘¥ At Damage Resolution End.
â‘  At Damage Resolution Start: [Gale], [Great Fog] effects (prevent damage) can execute.

â‘¡ When dealing damage: [Bare-Chested] (+1 damage), [Fathers' Legacyâ‘¡], [Arrow from the Dark], [Piercing Heart] and other general skills, plus equipment skills [Kirin Bow], [Frost Blade], [Ancient Scimitar] can activate.

â‘¢ When receiving damage: [Benevolent Heart], [Celestial Fragrance], [Haughty Pride], [Noble Scholar] and others, plus [Rattan Armourâ‘¡], [Silver Lion Helmetâ‘ ], [Talisman of Peaceâ‘ ] can activate.
â‘£ After dealing damage: [Set Aside Grudges], [Bloodthirst], [Blazing Blade], [Shatter the Army] and other general skills, plus [Three-Point Glaive] can activate.
â‘¤ After receiving damage: [Treacherous Hero], [Retribution], [Unyielding], [Posthumous Plan], [Preserve Lives], [Exile] and many other general skills can activate.
â‘¥ At Damage Resolution End: [Celestial Fragrance] draw effect executes; if the character was Chained and received elemental damage, they must Reset.

segments.en: draw effect executes; if the character was Chained and received elemental damage, they must Reset.

rules.en: After Damage Resolution: process in order: â… . [Wine and Verseâ‘¡]; â…¡. Trigger chain propagation (if applicable).
Since the final damage value is not yet determined at the "when dealing/receiving damage" timing, skills there default to triggering once per damage event regardless of the value.
"After receiving/dealing damage" defaults to once per damage event (regardless of value). If the damage value is X, skills with timing "after dealing/receiving 1 damage" can activate X times.

definition_en: Lose HP Resolution has two timings: â‘  When losing HP â‘¡ After losing HP.

rules.en: â‘  When losing HP: [Yellow Turban Talismanâ‘¡] (prevent HP loss) can activate. If the loss is prevented, resolution ends immediately; otherwise HP Reduction Resolution is conducted.

segments.en: (prevent HP loss) can activate. If prevented, resolution ends; otherwise HP Reduction Resolution is conducted.

rules.en: â‘¡ After losing HP: [Feigned Surrender] can activate.

definition_en: HP Reduction Resolution has three timings: â‘  Before HP is reduced â‘¡ When HP is reduced â‘¢ After HP is reduced. It can originate from either a Damage Event or a Lose HP Event.

rules.en: â‘  Before HP reduction (in a Damage Event): check activation conditions for [Wine and Verseâ‘¡], [Bloodthirst], [Tyranny], the Reset condition for characters receiving elemental damage, and the chain propagation condition.

segments.en: , Reset condition for elemental damage, and chain propagation condition.

rules.en: â‘¡ When HP is reduced: [Indomitable] can activate. HP is then reduced by the final value. If HP reaches 0, Verge of Death Resolution is conducted.

rules.en: â‘  Determining the recovery amount: [Rescue] (which modifies the recovery amount) can influence this. The character then recovers HP by the determined amount.

segments.en: (modifies recovery amount) can influence this. Character then recovers HP by the determined amount.

section_title_en: Reduce Max HP Event Resolution Flow
definition_en: Reduce Max HP Resolution has two timings: â‘  Before Max HP is reduced â‘¡ After Max HP is reduced.

rules.en: â‘  Before Max HP is reduced: no effect at this time. Max HP is then reduced. If the character's HP Value equalled their Max HP before the reduction, their HP Value is adjusted down to match.

segments.en: No effect. Max HP is then reduced. If HP Value equalled Max HP before, HP Value is adjusted down to match.

rules.en: â‘¡ After Max HP is reduced: a. If Max HP > 0: [Mourn the Fallen], [Imposing Presence] can activate. b. If Max HP = 0: that character dies.

section_title_en: Verge of Death Event Resolution Flow
definition_en: Verge of Death Resolution has two timings: â‘  When entering the Verge of Death State â‘¡ While in the Verge of Death State. One timing after: After Verge of Death Resolution.

rules.en: â‘  When entering the Verge of Death State: if a skill recovers A's HP Value to 1, A survives and resolution ends. [Supplement], [Follow the Situationâ‘ ] can activate.

segments.en: When entering Verge of Death State:
If a skill recovers A's HP to 1, A survives and resolution ends.

rules.en: â‘¡ While in the Verge of Death State: starting from the current turn's character going counter-clockwise, each character responds in sequence until A's HP reaches 1 or more (A survives). If A's HP is still 0 after the current turn character's Left Neighbor responds, A dies and Death Resolution is inserted. Skills such as [Nirvana], [Unyieldingâ‘ ], [Benevolent Heart] can activate. [Peach] and [Wine] can be used.

segments.en: While in Verge of Death State:

Starting from current turn's character, counter-clockwise, each responds until A's HP first reaches 1+ (A survives). If A's HP is still 0 after the current turn character's Left Neighbor responds, A dies and Death Resolution is inserted. Skills such as

examples.en: If B's response resolves and A has recovered some HP but not reached 1, A enters a new "While in the Verge of Death State" timing. The previous timing terminates and the sequence continues starting from B counter-clockwise.

rules.en: After Verge of Death Resolution: [Militarism] (lose 1 HP effect) can execute.

Skills that can activate at "While in the Verge of Death State" become Nullified after activating once, until the current Verge of Death Resolution ends. Each skill can only be used once per Verge of Death Resolution.

definition_en: Before Death Resolution: if the Lord dies, the game ends. Otherwise: if the character died from damage with a source, that source is treated as having killed them; if they died during a resolution in their own turn, their turn ends.

rules.en: Death Resolution has four timings (in order): â‘  Before revealing the Identity Card â‘¡ After revealing the Identity Card (check game-end condition) â‘¢ At the time of death â‘£ After death.

segments.en: After revealing Identity Card (check game-end condition)

rules.en: â‘  Before revealing the Identity Card: [Burn the Heart] can activate. The Identity Card is then revealed.

segments.en: can activate. Identity Card is then revealed.

rules.en: â‘¡ After revealing the Identity Card: check whether the game-ending condition has been met.

segments.en: Check whether the game-ending condition has been met.

rules.en: â‘¢ At the time of death: [Reap the Fallen], [Hidden Scheme], [Shed Tears], [In Memoriam], [Heartbreak], [Follow the Situationâ‘¡], [Spread Calamity], [War Soulâ‘¡] can activate. Then process in order: â… . System discards all cards in the dead character's zones and places cards on/beside their General Card into the Discard Pile; â…¡. Execute rewards/penalties (Lord kills Loyalist: Lord discards all cards; any character kills Rebel: draw 3 cards).

segments.en: can activate. Then process: â… . System discards all cards in dead character's zones; â…¡. Execute rewards/penalties (Lord kills Loyalist: Lord discards all; any kills Rebel: draw 3).

rules.en: â‘£ After death: general skills [Consolidate Resources], [Break the Encirclement], [Meritorious Valor], [Filial Virtue], and equipment skill [Soaring Dragon Bladeâ‘¡] can activate.
During Death Resolution, the dying character cannot activate any skills not listed in this resolution flow.
If a character dies during a resolution in their own turn, "After Turn End" only occurs after that event is fully resolved. The intervening period does not belong to any character's turn.

section_title_en: Topic 1: Conditions for Using a Card
definition_en: When a character needs to use a card, they can only use it (or activate a skill to use it) if no conditions exist that prevent them from doing so.

rules.en: Three reasons a character may be unable to use a card: â‘  A skill effect prevents it (e.g. [Iron Cavalry], [Chicken Rib], [Total Annihilation]); â‘¡ The frequency limit has been reached (mainly applies to [Kill]); â‘¢ There are no valid required targets.

segments.en: Three reasons a card cannot be used:
The frequency limit has been reached (mainly for
There are no valid required targets.

rules.en: Regarding [Kill] frequency limits: limits only apply when using [Kill] at an idle timing during the Action Phase. The default standard limit is 1; the default extra limit is 0. [Kill] uses count toward the standard limit first.

segments.en: frequency limits: limits only apply when using
at an idle timing during the Action Phase. The default standard limit is 1; the default extra limit is 0.
uses count toward the standard limit first.

examples.en: No frequency limit: [Repeating Crossbow], [Roar], [Spear Dance], [Charge the Phalanx] (win duel). Extra limit +1: [Cavalry Chargeâ‘¡], [Tiger's Roar], [Feigned Surrender], [Heaven's Justice] (win duel). Not counted: [Dragon Chant], [Spear Dance].

rules.en: Legality checks consist of two parts: distance legality (whether the distance requirement is met) and target legality (whether that character can be selected as a target).
When a character uses a physical card, the legality check treats the card as already placed into its target zone.

examples.en: When Zhuge Liang (Std) uses his last hand card [Proxy Attack], the legality check treats it as already in the Processing Zone â€” [Empty Fort] applies and he cannot target himself.

rules.en: There is a universal legality rule for Time-Delayed Tool Cards: a character who already has a Time-Delayed Tool Card of the same name in their Judgment Zone is not a valid target for another card of that same name.

section_title_en: Topic 2: Virtual Cards and Physical Cards
definition_en: Using, playing out, or discarding a card refers to operating a virtual card. The physical card that moves determines the virtual card's face information, but it is the virtual card â€” not the physical card â€” that is used, played, or discarded.

rules.en: There are four cases for how virtual and physical cards correspond: â‘  Normal use/play (not via "treated as"): one physical card maps to one virtual card. â‘¡ Via "treated as" use/play: the virtual card has no color, suit, or rank, with no corresponding physical card. â‘¢ Via "use card A as card B": one physical A card maps to one virtual B card. â‘£ Via "use multiple A cards as card B": multiple physical A cards collectively map to one virtual B card.

segments.en: Normal use/play: one physical card maps to one virtual card.
Via "treated as": virtual card has no color/suit/rank, no corresponding physical card.
Via "use card A as card B": one physical A maps to one virtual B.
Via "use multiple A cards as card B": multiple physical A cards map to one virtual B.

rules.en: When a skill operates on a virtual card, it defaults to operating on the corresponding physical card(s). If multiple physical cards correspond, all must be in their expected zones at execution time, otherwise the effect cannot execute. If one physical card corresponds, it must be in its expected zone. If no physical card corresponds, the effect cannot execute.

examples.en: Xiahou Yuan activates [Lightning Speedâ‘¡] using a virtual [Kill] on Cao Cao (Std). After Cao Cao receives the damage, since this [Kill] has no corresponding physical card, Cao Cao cannot activate [Treacherous Hero].

rules.en: Concepts in skills â€” such as "if this [Kill] has dealt damage," "the card that dealt damage," "channel," and "used/played/discarded a card" â€” all refer to the virtual card.

segments.en: Concepts in skills â€” such as "if this
has dealt damage," "the card that dealt damage," "channel," and "used/played/discarded a card" â€” all refer to the virtual card.
